history.note: Research approved after a max-effort adversarial verification. Outputs: research/2026-06-30-core-run-controls-research.md (8 open questions), research/2026-06-30-claude-code-usage-reference.md (reference deep-dive), research/2026-06-30-verification-addendum.md (3 independent verifiers vs real code + SDK .d.ts — 0 claims refuted; proved AbortSignal.any() type-checks under lib:[ES2022] via real tsc; 9 engineering precision notes). User decision locked in decisions.md: token-usage surface = Option A (cumulative usage on terminal events + Terminal, normalized cross-provider shape, merge/accumulate helpers), mirroring Claude Code; Terminal.usage is the sub-agent Task-tool roll-up seam.

Engineering spec approved: engineering/2026-06-30-core-run-controls-engineering.md (+ spec-review-addendum, RESOLVED). All 8 open questions resolved; 11 decisions logged. Final review by 2 independent reviewers (adversarial code-sketch review + blast-radius audit) vs real code + SDK types + a real tsc compile (mergeUsage/accumulateUsage/translateChunk exit 0; per-turn accumulator isolation confirmed; all 9 precision notes honored). 4 findings folded into the spec: (1 blocking) compile-breaking typed-literal terminals in collect.test.ts/types.test.ts need usage:EMPTY_USAGE; (2) pin turnUsage to top of while body; (3) message_stop usage conditional on BOTH providers (asymmetry fix); (4) test dir + nits. Design: external AbortSignal via AbortSignal.any + pre-flight guard; normalized Usage in types/usage.ts; usage on terminal events+Terminal (non-optional, EMPTY_USAGE fallback) + turn_complete (optional); message_stop ProviderEvent +usage?; OpenAI include_usage always-on. Additive; regression bar 196.
